nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Eatington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Edington
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the three places called Edington in Northumberland, Somerset, and Wiltshire, or from Eddington, Berkshire. Edington in Northumberland arises from Old English Idingtūn, from the Old English personal name Ida, + ing, denoting association, + tūn ‘settlement’ (compare Ida ). In Somerset, the name comes either from the Old English male name Ēadwine or from the feminine Ēadwynn + ing + tūn. Edington (Wiltshire) is from either the Old English personal name Ētha + dūn ‘hill’, or from Old English ēthe ‘uncultivated’ + dūn.

nameMeanings.name: Jordan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a name given to a child of either sex baptized in holy water that was, purportedly at least, brought from the River Jordan, whose Hebrew name, ha-yarden, means ‘flowing down’. It was in this river that Christ was baptized by John the Baptist, and medieval pilgrims to the Holy Land usually tried to bring back a flask of its water with them. The modern given name is either a revival of this, or else a transferred use of the surname that was derived from the medieval given name. It is more popular as a boy's name in Britain and as a girl's name in the United States.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Jordan de Eatington was born about 1134, in Ettington, Warwickshire, England, his father, Fulcher de Eatington, was 40 and his mother, Mrs Fulcher de Eatington, was 37. He had at least 3 sons with Mrs. Jordan de Eatington.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jordan d’Eatington est né environ 1134, à Ettington, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Fulcher d’Eatington, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Mrs Fulcher d’Eatington, avait 37 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Mrs. Jordan d’Eatington.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jordan de Eatington um 1134 in Ettington, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Fulcher de Eatington 40 und seine Mutter Mrs Fulcher de Eatington 37. Er hatte mit Mrs. Jordan de Eatington mindestens 3 Söhne.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jordan de Eatington è nato il circa 1134 nel Ettington, Warwickshire, England, il suo padre, Fulcher de Eatington, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Mrs Fulcher de Eatington, aveva 37 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Mrs. Jordan de Eatington.
